description_long: This is a weathered, industrial fire hydrant with a domed top and a cylindrical body, painted in a faded yellow color with visible rust and grime, suggesting long-term outdoor exposure. The hydrant features two prominent side outlets, each capped and secured with metal chains, and a larger central outlet on the rear, also capped and rusted. The surface is rough and pitted, with chipped paint and corrosion especially around the base and outlet areas. The top of the hydrant has a hexagonal operating nut, used for opening the valve to release water. The overall form is robust and utilitarian, designed to withstand harsh urban environments and provide emergency water access. No manufacturer or model markings are visible on the exterior.
description: A weathered yellow fire hydrant with rusted caps, metal chains, and a domed top, showing signs of age and outdoor exposure.
description_view_0: Front view showing two side outlets with caps and chains, and the domed top with visible rust and grime.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the large central outlet cap and chain, with less detail on the side outlets.
description_view_2: Similar to the front, this view shows both side outlets and chains, with more visible surface wear and rust.
description_view_3: Rear angle focusing on the central outlet cap and chain, with the domed top and weathered surface visible.
